subject: History
chapter_name: Weavers, Iron Smelters and Factory Owners
overall_learning_objectives: Understand the importance of Indian textile production before British rule
Explain how British policies affected Indian weavers
Understand the decline of Indian handloom industry
Learn about the work of iron smelters in India
Understand the rise of factory production in India
Analyse the role of Indian entrepreneurs in industrial development

lesson_plan.title: Indian Textile Production in the 18th Century
topics_covered: Famous Indian textiles such as muslin and silk
Demand for Indian cloth in international markets
student_activities: List famous types of Indian cloth
Discuss why Indian textiles were popular worldwide

lesson_plan.title: Impact of British Policies on Weavers
topics_covered: Role of the East India Company in textile trade
student_activities: Write how the Company controlled weavers

lesson_plan.title: Decline of the Handloom Industry
topics_covered: Loss of markets for Indian cloth
student_activities: List reasons for the decline of Indian weaving

lesson_plan.title: Iron Smelting in India
student_activities: Write how iron was produced traditionally
Discuss problems faced by iron smelters

lesson_plan.title: Rise of Factory Production
student_activities: Identify differences between handloom and factory production

lesson_plan.title: Indian Entrepreneurs and Chapter Revision
student_activities: Write one example of an Indian entrepreneur

teaching_aids: Pictures of traditional weaving and factories
teacher_tip: Use visual examples of handloom weaving and factory machines to help students clearly understand the transition from traditional crafts to industrial production.
